name: Fabric (Python SSH Deployment)

auth.notes: SSH key or password auth via Paramiko; SSH agent forwarding supported

performance.notes: SSH connection overhead; parallel execution with Group; depends on network and command

agent_readiness.notes: Python library for SSH-based deployment and remote command execution. Connection object with run(), sudo(), put(), get(). ThreadingGroup for parallel multi-host execution. Local() for local commands. Invoke integration for task definitions (fabfile.py). Streaming output. Context managers for connection lifecycle. Python 3 only in Fabric 2+. Replaces shell scripts for deployment automation. Simpler than Ansible for Python teams.

gotchas: Fabric 2/3 is different from legacy Fabric 1.x (not backward compatible)
No idempotency guarantees — must implement yourself
SSH connection management can be tricky for long-running operations
best_when: SSH-based server deployment from Python scripts
Parallel command execution across multiple hosts
Teams preferring Python over shell scripts or Ansible
summary: Fabric is a Python SSH deployment library built on Paramiko. Run remote commands, transfer files, and execute local tasks via a clean Python API. Connection.run(), .sudo(), .put(), .get(). ThreadingGroup for parallel multi-host operations. fabfile.py for task definitions with Invoke integration. Simpler than Ansible for Python teams with straightforward SSH automation needs.
